content:
[S1]: Have you ever felt really warm on a sunny day and wondered why?  
[READER]: Curious and interested because they know about sunny days and warmth. They want to learn why it happens. Next sentence should start explaining how the sun’s heat works.

[S2]: The sun sends light and heat to Earth, which helps plants grow and keeps us warm.  
[READER]: Understands the sun’s role but might wonder what happens after the heat reaches Earth. Next sentence should introduce what happens to the heat next.

[S3]: Some of the heat goes back into space, but some gets trapped by gases in the air called greenhouse gases.  
[READER]: A little confused about gases trapping heat. Wants to know what greenhouse gases are and why they trap heat. Next sentence should explain greenhouse gases simply.

[S4]: Greenhouse gases are like a big, invisible blanket around Earth that holds in the heat.  
[READER]: Imagining a blanket and starting to understand. Curious about what makes this blanket thicker or thinner. Next sentence should explain why more gases make it warmer.

[S5]: When people burn things like coal, oil, and gas, they add more greenhouse gases to the air.  
[READER]: Interested but maybe a little unsure about burning coal and oil. Might need examples. Next sentence should give easy examples.

[S6]: Cars, factories, and even some power plants burn these fuels and send extra gases into the sky.  
[READER]: Beginning to connect the idea with things they see around them. Wants to know what happens because of the extra gases. Next sentence should explain the effect.

[S7]: Because of this, the invisible blanket gets thicker and holds in more heat, making the Earth warmer.  
[READER]: Starting to understand why Earth is getting hotter. Curious about what that means for animals and plants. Next sentence should explain effects briefly.

[S8]: This warming can change weather, melt ice, and make it harder for some animals and plants to live.  
[READER]: Feels concerned and wants to know what people can do to help. Next sentence should give a hopeful message.

[S9]: But people are working on using cleaner energy and planting trees to help cool the Earth down.  
[READER]: Feels hopeful and wants to learn more about how they can help. The explanation feels complete and positive.

===BEGIN===
Have you ever felt really warm on a sunny day and wondered why? The sun sends light and heat to Earth, which helps plants grow and keeps us warm. Some of the heat goes back into space, but some gets trapped by gases in the air called greenhouse gases. Greenhouse gases are like a big, invisible blanket around Earth that holds in the heat. When people burn things like coal, oil, and gas, they add more greenhouse gases to the air. Cars, factories, and even some power plants burn these fuels and send extra gases into the sky. Because of this, the invisible blanket gets thicker and holds in more heat, making the Earth warmer. This warming can change weather, melt ice, and make it harder for some animals and plants to live. But people are working on using cleaner energy and planting trees to help cool the Earth down.
===END===
